news.title: NZ international enrolments hit 92k as recovery gathers pace
news.summary_en: New Zealand’s international education recovery is accelerating, with enrolments climbing 11% year-on-year to reach 92,580 students in 2025, according to new figures from Education New Zealand (ENZ).
news.author: Kim Martin

news.title: ICE director brands OPT a “magnet for fraud”
news.summary_en: The head of America’s notorious Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has called the US post-study work program a “magnet for fraud”, vowing to “relentlessly investigate” students and employers.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: Revealed: the best-performing agents for New Zealand
news.summary_en: Immigration New Zealand has published the top-performing agents for nine source countries based on application volume and visa approval rates. The data also reveals a higher visa approval rate for agent-linked applications than in previous years.
news.author: The PIE News team

news.title: US phases out international student crisis protections
news.summary_en: The US government has quietly phased out a policy that provided regulatory exemptions for students from regions marred by war, natural disaster or financial crises.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: MPs warn UK university insolvency risk grows amid international student pressures
news.summary_en: UK MPs have raised concerns over new visa compliance metrics, the international student levy and lack of government planning for a potential major university collapse.
news.author: Kim Martin

news.title: India seeks database of outbound students amid global conflicts
news.summary_en: Amid the Russia-Ukraine war and recent Middle East tensions, India’s ministry of external affairs is reportedly planning a dedicated platform for Indians going abroad for education and employment to strengthen emergency coordination.
news.author: Abhishek Nair

news.title: Global HE enrolment reaches 269 million as student mobility triples
news.summary_en: UNESCO’s first Higher Education Global Trends Report highlights rapid growth in global participation and student mobility, even as inequalities, affordability pressures and quality concerns persist.
news.author: Abhishek Nair

news.title: Universities urged to “think differently” as half report enrolment declines
news.summary_en: A survey of 254 universities across 36 countries has found a significant decline in international enrolments, with institutions pointing to restrictive policies and visa issues as key factors.
news.author: Kim Martin

news.title: Canada overhauls immigration consultant regulations
news.summary_en: The Canadian government has announced new rules for immigration consultants including increased penalties, an expanded public register and compensation for victims of dishonest acts.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: Emerging Futures 9: visa uncertainty reshapes student demand globally
news.summary_en: More international students are ruling out study destinations earlier because of visa uncertainty, costs and a lack of clarity about what an application will involve, according to new global research from IDP Education.
news.author: Kim Martin

news.title: Study Group sold to Arete Education
news.summary_en: Study Group has been acquired by Arete Education, the higher education venture backed by Global University Systems (GUS) and Brightstar Capital Partners.
news.author: Kim Martin
